justification: All pass criteria met: CAP theorem tension explicitly identified in executive_summary and trade_offs[0]. Escalations ask product team to clarify availability SLA priority. Design clearly states consistency is chosen over availability (CP), with 5-15s failover window as the sacrifice. Team size constraint deeply factored throughout — PostgreSQL over distributed DB, monolith over microservices, managed services to reduce ops burden. trade_offs[0] explicitly names the CAP trade-off with detailed alternatives considered.

observations.text: CAP theorem identified immediately in executive_summary and thoroughly explained in trade_offs with CP vs AP framing
Escalation clearly asks product to define availability SLA before committing, with concrete options tied to team size
Design explicitly states consistency is prioritized — 5-15s unavailability during failover is the named sacrifice
Team size constraint is a first-class concern: drives PostgreSQL choice, monolith recommendation, managed services, and rejection of Raft/CockroachDB
SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED is an excellent practical choice with clear rationale
Alternatives considered section in trade_offs thoroughly evaluates AP, distributed consensus, and Redis options with specific rejection reasons
Confidence level of 58 is well-calibrated given the unstated SLA and throughput requirements
Pitfalls section demonstrates deep domain knowledge (Kleppmann's Redlock analysis, async anti-pattern for latency-sensitive path)
